puzzle.title: The Burden of a Promise
puzzle.intro: In the annals of faith, some names are mentioned as a testament to a single, profound quality. Today's figure is one such soul, whose very name is tied to a pledge of steadfastness. This individual is honored in the Quran for a character trait that serves as a lesson for all of humanity, earning a place among the most righteous. Can you uncover the identity of this paragon of patience and commitment?
clues: My story in the Quran is brief, yet my name is mentioned twice, listed among the patient and the righteous.
I am honored in one chapter alongside Ismail and Idris, remembered for our collective steadfastness.
My name itself is a title, signifying 'one of the portion' or 'one who took on a charge or guarantee'.
Allah explicitly states that He entered me into His mercy because I was truly among the righteous.
In another chapter, I am mentioned with Ismail and Al-Yasa' (Elisha) as one of the chosen and best.
I am Dhul-Kifl, and my entire Quranic legacy is captured in my unwavering patience and righteousness.

trial.label: What is my defining characteristic in the Quran?

location.label: I am mentioned alongside whom in Surah Al-Anbiya?

outcome.label: What was my reward mentioned in the Quran?
options: Being saved from a great fire
Being taught the speech of birds

puzzle.arabic: وَإِسْمَـٰعِيلَ وَإِدْرِيسَ وَذَا ٱلْكِفْلِ ۖ كُلٌّۭ مِّنَ ٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ
puzzle.verse: And [mention] Ishmael and Idrees and Dhul-Kifl; all were of the patient. (21:85)
